name: Fashion Lookbook
description: Editorial fashion lookbook video — 15-30s of model + garment storytelling: hook (0-2s) → look 1 walk/pose → optional transition into look 2/3 → final beat. 9:16 vertical (TikTok/Reels) by default, 16:9 for campaign films. Movement sells clothes — fabric drape, model walks, lighting that flatters the wearer, environment as mood. Reference-required when a real garment is shown: drop a product/garment photo into uploaded/, or the format refuses (no AI-improvised fashion branding). Music carries the rhythm; voiceover is usually omitted in favor of on-screen text and beat-cut editing.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter, multi-ref WITH user-supplied garment photo (required for any frame where a real branded piece is visible). 1080x1920 vertical, 5-6 keyframes typical (one per look + transition anchor).
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips. 3-6 clips depending on look count and transitions. generate_audio: false. Camera cues: power-walk, slow rotation, fabric-wind, macro-pull-back.
stackSummary.voiceover: USUALLY OMITTED. Fashion lookbooks live on rhythm, not narration. If VO is requested (campaign style), one ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 line, calm/editorial delivery, max 2 sentences.
stackSummary.captions: Minimal — brand name + collection tag, on-screen text only. No HormoziCaptions (too aggressive for fashion). MinimalCaptions or no captions at all.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single track, mood matched to category (cinematic-orchestral for haute couture, hip-hop for streetwear, indie/lo-fi for sustainable, electronic for contemporary). BPM drives transition cuts.

constants.structure: 2s hook → look(s) walk/turn/detail → final pose / brand beat

variationAxes: category (haute couture, streetwear, athleisure, formal, casual, swimwear, accessories, sustainable, vintage, evening)
era / season / aesthetic (Y2K, 90s minimalism, dark academia, cottagecore, futurism, archival)
location (studio white-cyc, urban street, rooftop, garden, beach, industrial warehouse, gallery, hotel lobby, desert)
lighting (golden hour, studio flash, bright daylight, neon night, soft window, dramatic backlight, gel-color)
fabric focus (silk, denim, leather, chiffon, knit, velvet, sequins, lace, technical, fur)
look count (single look 15s vs 3-look 15s vs 5+ look rapid-fire)
music mood (cinematic-orchestral, hip-hop, indie, pop-dance, lo-fi, electronic, acoustic, retro)
doNotCopyLiterally: Specific outfits, model identity, brand names, exact prompts. Vibe reference only — adapt to the user's brand, garment, and category.
referenceNotes: Garment / brand reference (product photo of the actual piece, fabric swatch, lookbook still, or brand mood-board) is REQUIRED whenever a real branded item is shown. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) refuses if no reference exists at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. Generic fashion (no specific brand, fictional collection) MAY proceed without a reference — log as stage: 'no-ref-consent' and warn the user the AI-improvised garment will not match any real product. Model identity is a separate concern — if a named person is requested, that is also reference-gated.
